The multiplayer in this game is great, it’s just that there’s not many people in multiplayer servers. Overall, I would buy this game again if my meta account got deleted.

I got Stilt yesterday while it was on sale, and my first impression was really good. It has a decent story line, the graphics are really good, and the movement is harder than I first thought but adds a new level of challenge to it. The levels don’t have time limits so you can take as much time as you want to explore and complete it at your own pace. Also for people wondering (I know I was) the stilts are extensions of your arms that hang off and aren’t part of your actual arm.

This game is super fun and the way you move in the game is really unique, I would recommend for new vr players only thing is I wish  is if the game was free and there was public story mode servers that was always open cuz the fact you need to get on the game at a certain time so u can play pubs is a bit boring and I wish you could buy in game currency to buy the items all of those reasons  is what takes a star from my review.

Edit, after several month: 

Still a wonderful game. And one of the only two games I play regularly with my friend and others from over the world. I hope, there will be more players to play with in Multiplayer.

First rating:

I didn't expect this first, but I like the game very much and Multiplayer is a lot of fun. 😄 I really would appreciate some DLC for multiplayer, more areas like the castle like lobby, maybe larger ones... I'd pay for it.😃

So glad I stumbled onto this game. It’s one of the best games I own on quest. I loved No More Rainbows but this outshines it in every respect…. Even the movement feels more precise.  Reminds me of Mario 64 with NMR controls

Update 2/23/25: it’s an absolute crime this game hasn’t sold better. This is honestly a top 10 quest game, and I own over 700 titles, some free but mostly paid.  There’s something seriously wrong with the meta store when a game like this can’t get more exposure.  

Really hope the devs decide to make a sequel or follow up… wouldn’t blame them in the slightest if they decided it’s not worth it though.  But don’t you worry, the meta store will advertise the 827th gorilla tag clone so you can always play that garbage.

This game has gone under the radar somehow and I it is a shame because it is excellent. It revisits the genre of gorilla platforming like No More Rainbows did and it has its own innovations that make it shine on its own. The locomotion is more forgiving than your average GT because gravity is forgiving and the stilts give you a lot of force to jump far if you wish. The tools you attach add new dimensions to the locomotion: the propeller, the swinging rope, the booster (if that's the name). I enjoy exploring the map trying to get to places I'm not supposed to visit and it is great fun. The game also offers different levels of difficulty without you knowing: you can "easily" complete the levels but if you get a bit OCD and want to speed-run them or get to the hidden loot you have to work hard. I'm some 12 h or so into the game and I'm on level 12 so you can tell how much replayability the levels provide if you enjoy playing. Finally, if you like the VR experiences that can be a bit of a workout without you noticing, this is one of them. I play it wearing wrist weights and get quite tired after 30 minutes of playing.

Classic Mario-like level design, complete with main overworld and secret levels + physics based bouncy GT locomotion, but a lot more springy and coupled with great powerups...

fun (and challenge) for the whole family, especially experienced monkebois
I did not like this game, it is good just not good for mature players, you should only get this game if its free or for your children it has good graphics tho

reviews.review_title: A review by a critical thinker
reviews.review_description:
This game is precisely the next step in the evolution of platformers that we need. The immersion and 1:1 movement of your hands and head is the biggest leap in platformers since Mario 64. And Stilt fully utilizes the potential of this evolution. The physics and the feel of the movement feel so good that I wouldn't even know if they could be improved upon. This is 100% quality innovative platforming gameplay, even on just the Quest 2.
And what makes it better is the fact that you actually have to learn and get used to the physics, which is what VR desperately needs after years of hand hold-y and mashing gameplay of most VR games, which is still seen even in recent releases.

What's not so quality is the presentation. While the graphics are fine, everything feels too shovelwareish. Sadly the visual design doesn't remind me of those old simple nostalgic 3D graphics, but more so just basic forgettable mobile games' style. I can't put my finger on it, but it's missing that something that would make it not feel soulless.

On top of that, the design of the main character is atrocious. And since that's what you see on the store page, alongside with the unexciting name "Stilt", many will no doubt not even bother checking out the game at all, which is such a shame. 
Despite it's visual design, this game is one of the best games on VR, and it's truly a shame that it's so hard for people to discover.

A few more great things to mention are the fun powerups, level design Gameplay-wise, and there's even online multiplayer.
With more content apparently on the way, this game is absolutely worth the 20 bucks.
How do you make a game with such crapy movement and absolutely trash physics and multiplayer that doesn’t even work

developer_response.body:
Sorry to hear the game didn’t click with you. The movement style is actually something a lot of players have praised, but we understand it’s not for everyone.
As for multiplayer, we haven’t seen any widespread issues recently. If you ran into something specific, feel free to let us know so we can take a closer look. Appreciate you giving it a try either way!

reviews.review_title: one of the BEST vr games
reviews.review_description:
i’ve been following the development of this game for awhile and was super excited for it, and it lived up to the hype! if you’ve played the demo for this game it’s just that but more of it and they improve on a lot of stuff, and a multiplayer mode.

i love vr platformers and i’ve tried a ton of them but this one is defintely my favorite, the main campaign is so long and fun and then if you want you can replay it all and try to get the 5 presents every time, and then if you still want more content you can try multiplayer which has 5 different modes i think and offers infinite replay ability, this game is great and really deserves more attentio
I love the graphics and everything ￼
Have you always wondered what it's like to have springs for legs? Now you can. This is probably one of the funnest experiences I've had in virtual reality. It's a very simple game and lots of fun!

I am relatively new to VR and the mode of locomotion through the game play on stilts was fun. I appreciate all the little details in the graphics and getting used to to using my arms to get around the course. 

I think this will be fun to play w friends once I play in multiplayer mode. Fun for families and friends to play together. Jumping from place to place. It takes getting used to if you haven't played a game using your arms as the means of "walking". Special thanks to the devs for making this great game.

reviews.review_title: One of the best platformers I've played
reviews.review_description: This is an amazing fully fleshed out platforming game with a bunch of original mechanics. And it seems sort of massive just from glancing at the overworld. Best part is it's easy to get in to because the locomotion is so good but the skill cieling is also realy high. Every level has timed medals that force you to think out side the box and perfect jumps to get the best times.

Very easy to get the hang off, game for all ages, I’m 47 and like it, my son plays it and loves it. Lots to collect and to do as you go through the levels working your way to the boss or big pink octopus from what I can see. I don’t want to give to much away but it fun. 

The graphics’s are very good ￼and you will play it for hours.

reviews.review_title: Absolutely the Best VR Experience I've
reviews.review_description:
Stilt is an absolute masterpiece! The movement and immersion in the virtual world are unlike anything I've experienced before. The blend of platforming, reflex challenges, and creative problem-solving is incredibly well executed, making every moment thrilling.

The stilt mechanics are intuitive and satisfying, and I found myself completely lost in the adventure, crossing gaps, smashing obstacles, and discovering awesome power-ups. And the multiplayer modes? Just pure fun! Battling friends in Smack-em-all or holding the zone in Area Bash brought a new level of competitive thrill.

Hands down, Stilt is the best VR game I've played. If you're looking for fantastic movement, fun challenges, and an amazing multiplayer experience, this is it. Strap on those stilts and have a blast!

reviews.review_title: Totally Great in single player, not coop
reviews.review_description: PEOPLE JOIN MULTIPLAYER, IT'S FUN, PLS IM LONELY

reviews.review_title: This game is a hidden gem
reviews.review_description: loving everything about this game so far + you get free exercise from playing it :P I'm only 10 levels deep but the difficulty has ramped up perfectly, not too easy and not too difficult, the perfect amount to deliver a challenge that is satisfying to overcome. If you enjoy platformers then I for sure recommend giving this game a try.

so i was skeptical about this game when i saw it.
i later got it on a deal then sat on it for a while.
and here i am today writing a small review.
i played through the first few worlds so its not a complet review.
but i did enjoy what i have played. 
the movement mechanic is interesting and novel compared to others games. i trid gorila tag and didnt get on with it. i tried this and its clear the dev team understand how to do it better.
no motion sickness !!!!!! and its always one of my big worries with VR titles.
the silts are a bit irritating when your to close to an object.. and push me away (as it would in life as well) this isnt a complaint! just a mechanic i need to learn. 
its a nice cozy chilld game with a bit of excersies also.
its fun bouncing around collecting coins and when you get a good rythem going its rewarding,
good job Dev team, its a fun title differant and i would recemend to others. hope this works out for you.

I picked this game when it was on sale for fairly cheap, as I thought it was just another kid's game. To my surprise, the visual quality and the gameplay are leagues better than I expected.

The simple but deep gameplay is satisfying, in this game is a perfect entry point for introducing new people to VR. 

Kudos to the developers!

reviews.review_title: Worth pushing past the motion sickness.
reviews.review_description: This is a hidden gem of a game that I highly recommend with friends. It really does test your VR legs though. If you are new to VR, approach this game in small doses to ease into it without motion sickness.

reviews.review_title: easier to get sick than Gorilla Tag
reviews.review_description:
(updated review after playing a 3rd time, 2 hours total)
Finally found other players. Couldn't get into the Discord event because I was too late, but there were 5 players in the lobby -- not nearly as many as Gorilla Tag, but not bad. It was really fun. The server region setting is confusing -- the players in the lobby seem to be the same after I switched from EU to US East (and exit and reentered).

(updated review after playing a 2nd time, 1.5 hours total)
Almost no motion sickness this time, maybe due to familiarity or better techniques. The bouncy balls still too easily get caught inside objects (collision bugs?) or unintentionally bounce off walls. Levels are more interesting and varied than I thought before. The maps are nonlinear, with lots of secrets. Learning curve is gradual and deep. Still no players online (tried us-west and eu, though both not at night or weekend, haven't tried Discord events).

(original review)
The bounciness in your stilts greatly smooths your motion and makes your control much less responsive -- for someone with quick reflexes it's a big drawback. It also makes you mini-bounce around 5 times each time you try to stop, which means 5 times as much motion sickness. It's also too easy to accidentally bounce off walls due to your long arms. Every unexpected motion adds to your motion sickness. Familiarity helps, and it takes several times longer than GTag to become familiar. Graphics wise it's very pleasant, though each level only adds a little newness. There seems to be barely any other players online.

Overall, my main complaint is the discomfort and loneliness.

developer_response.body:
Thank you for the thoughtful review and for giving us a 4-star rating despite the challenges you experienced with the locomotion. We understand that the stilt-based movement offers a unique experience that can take some time to adjust to. While many players enjoy the style, we recognize that it may not be a perfect fit for everyone, and we appreciate your feedback on the motion sickness.

For the multiplayer experience, we invite you to join our community events, like the Sunday multiplayer sessions on Discord. It's a great way to connect with other players and enjoy the social side of the game. Also, trying out different server regions can help you find more active players.

Thanks again for sharing your thoughts—your feedback is incredibly helpful as we continue to expand and refine the game!

reviews.review_title: So much more fun than I expected!
reviews.review_description:
Who would have thought that jumping on stilts would be one of the greatest ways of locomotion in VR? It's incredible fun to swing your arms around to move your character through the levels. I'm a little bit too old to play Gorilla Tag, so without Stilt I'd never have known :) The movement just feels fantastic and becomes second nature to you. The levels are varied, well designed, the art-style (which is definitely inspired by all-time classics like Super Mario) is also nice and clean. It used to be quite challenging, but an update added an Assist mode to the game that allows you to customize the difficulty level perfectly to your liking!

I recommend this game to anyone who has always wanted to be IN a platformer instead of just controlling a character in third person. And a very special recommendation goes out to people who like speedrunning, this game is made for that!

I am enjoying this game so far:) seems like it would be super easy but it does have moments where it’s super challenging as well. Graphics are bright and fun and well done. Would recommend to add to your library.
Swinging your arms around and bouncing around the levels is quite fun. Gives your arms a nice workout too.
Definitely a unique platformer, had me going back to the N64 days!
This is the best game ever you need to buy it
I can’t get through one part so please make it so you only had to attempt each level

developer_response.body: Hey there, thanks a ton for loving Stilt! 🎉 We designed some tricky spots to keep things interesting, but we know they can be a real challenge. Have you given the new Assist Mode a whirl yet? It’s like having a secret weapon in your VR toolkit, letting you tweak the difficulty so you can glide through those tougher parts with ease. Give it a go, and let us know how it helps you conquer those levels!

reviews.review_description:
Good game to play but there are some issues 

1.way to difficult I don't know if it's just me or what but it is to difficult 

2. Everything kills you 3 hits which is way to low

3. Water and lava 1 hits you like I get lava a little bit  but Water just let me swim man or make it so ther is a time to get your self out of it before you die

developer_response.body:
We're delighted you enjoyed playing the game and appreciate your feedback. 
The game is difficult, but we are planning on adding an easy mode to ensure that everyone can fully enjoy the experience. Thank you for your valuable input!

reviews.review_description:
I pretty much love every thing about this game and everything about it
1. During the main games story mode you can pretty much throughout the whole thing find about 3 enemies, (that I know of) the squid, pufferfish thing, and the bird, and in my opinion sometimes the characters lack a lot.
2. Ok, this might just be me, but the lava is ANNOYING, and so is the water. Idk if I missed something but how come when you dip your stilt which probably I don’t think is part of your body, you die? Now for lava, same thing, I get it’s lava but I think it should be like you have a few seconds to react to being in the lava. Maybe something like every second in lava, you take damage? I think that would be good.
3. All the levels kind of lack gimmicks, all of the ones I can make right off the bat are platforms, clouds, abilities, lava/water, and probably my most favourite is to being able to change size from I think level 16.
Over all, I think this game is solid, I love the cartoony aspect and the mix of one of my favourite vr games, gorilla tag, really blends in with an amazing story mode.
Ps and also kind of spoilers: I don’t really know what to do on the last phase of the boss fight, do you just make a run for it to the ship?
With amazing power ups and levels that keep you on your feet if you are looking for a challenging vr platformer I couldn’t recommend you a better game

The ratings don’t lie, this game is amazing, 
No platform game on the Quest like it, so colourful, loving the bounce mechanics, I’m trying to bounce so high just for fun.
Swinging my arms has never been so much fun.
Still have lots of hours left, but so far I can’t find anything bad to say.
Well done to Petter and REKT Games, Max Mustard definitely has a platform rival.
Just Brilliant.

This game is AMAZING. The platforming is fun, the power ups are so cool to mess around with, and there’s tons of secrets to find. There’s one secret in the last leveled and it’s just a room full of hearts. Fun game highly recommend you buy
